message:
feat: add onboarding lessons for 6 new stack tools

Create lesson components for fsfs, sbh, casr, dsr, asb, and pcr
with feature grids, command lists, and usage examples. Register
all lessons in index.tsx and lessons.ts with proper slugs.

- fsfs-lesson.tsx: Hybrid search with BM25 + semantic retrieval
- sbh-lesson.tsx: Disk pressure defense with ballast files
- casr-lesson.tsx: Cross-provider session resumption
- dsr-lesson.tsx: Local release builds when CI is throttled
- asb-lesson.tsx: Multi-agent config backup and restore
- pcr-lesson.tsx: Post-compaction AGENTS.md re-read hook
- Close 9 onboarding lesson beads in issues.jsonl
- Enable no-db mode in beads config (SQLite corruption workaround)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -11,7 +11,7 @@ issue-prefix: "bd"
 # Use no-db mode: load from JSONL, no SQLite, write back after each command
 # When true, bd will use .beads/issues.jsonl as the source of truth
 # instead of SQLite database
-# no-db: false
+no-db: true
 
 # Disable daemon for RPC communication (forces direct database access)
 # no-daemon: false

@@ -1629,7 +1629,6 @@ modules:
     verified_installer:
       tool: ru
       runner: bash
-      args: ["--easy-mode"]
     install: []
     verify:
       - ru --version

@@ -497,6 +497,26 @@ fi
 # MCP Agent Mail helper (installer usually adds `am`, but keep a fallback)
 alias am='cd ~/mcp_agent_mail 2>/dev/null && scripts/run_server_with_token.sh || echo "mcp_agent_mail not found in ~/mcp_agent_mail"'
 
+# --- ACFS tool aliases (new tools) ---
+# RCH: offload cargo/gcc builds to remote workers
+command -v rch &>/dev/null && alias rb='rch exec -- cargo build --release'
+command -v rch &>/dev/null && alias rt='rch exec -- cargo test'
+# FrankenSearch
+command -v fsfs &>/dev/null && alias fs='fsfs search'
+# Process Triage
+command -v pt &>/dev/null && alias ptop='pt top'
+# Storage Ballast Helper
+command -v sbh &>/dev/null && alias sbs='sbh status'
+# Cross-Agent Session Resumer
+command -v casr &>/dev/null && alias resume='casr resume'
+# Doodlestein Self-Releaser
+command -v dsr &>/dev/null && alias release='dsr check --all'
+# Agent Settings Backup
+command -v asb &>/dev/null && alias backup='asb backup --all'
+# Post-Compact Reminder (no alias needed, runs as a hook)
+# Repo Updater
+command -v ru &>/dev/null && alias sync='ru sync'
+
 # --- Keybindings (quality of life) ---
 # Ctrl+Arrow for word movement
 bindkey "^[[1;5C" forward-word

@@ -0,0 +1,107 @@ +'use client'; + +import { + Terminal, + Save, + FolderSync, + Clock, + Play, + Shield, +} from 'lucide-react'; +import { + Section, + Paragraph, + CodeBlock, + TipBox, + Highlight, + Divider, + GoalBanner, + CommandList, + FeatureCard, + FeatureGrid, +} from './lesson-components'; + +export function AsbLesson() { + return ( +
+ + Back up and restore your AI agent configurations with Agent Settings Backup. + + + {/* Section 1: What Is ASB */} +
} delay={0.1}> + + ASB (Agent Settings Backup) is a smart backup tool + for AI coding agent configuration folders. It backs up settings for Claude Code, + Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and other agents to a single versioned archive. + + + When you provision a new machine or recover from a misconfiguration, ASB + restores all your agent settings, skills, hooks, and preferences in one command. + + +
+ + } + title="Multi-Agent Backup" + description="Claude, Cursor, Codex, Gemini configs in one archive" + gradient="from-emerald-500/20 to-green-500/20" + /> + } + title="One-Command Restore" + description="Restore all settings to a new machine instantly" + gradient="from-blue-500/20 to-indigo-500/20" + /> + } + title="Versioned Snapshots" + description="Keep historical snapshots of your config state" + gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-yellow-500/20" + /> + } + title="Selective Restore" + description="Restore specific agents or specific config files" + gradient="from-purple-500/20 to-violet-500/20" + /> + +
+
+ + + + {/* Section 2: Essential Commands */} +
} delay={0.2}> + + + + Run ASB backup before provisioning new machines or making major configuration + changes to your agent setups. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 3: Common Scenarios */} +
} delay={0.3}> + +
+
+ ); +}

@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@ +'use client'; + +import { + Terminal, + Repeat, + FileText, + ArrowRightLeft, + Play, + Lightbulb, +} from 'lucide-react'; +import { + Section, + Paragraph, + CodeBlock, + TipBox, + Highlight, + Divider, + GoalBanner, + CommandList, + FeatureCard, + FeatureGrid, +} from './lesson-components'; + +export function CasrLesson() { + return ( +
+ + Resume coding sessions across AI providers with Cross-Agent Session Resumer. + + + {/* Section 1: What Is CASR */} +
} delay={0.1}> + + CASR (Cross-Agent Session Resumer) converts and resumes + AI coding sessions across different providers. Start a session in Claude Code, + continue it in Codex CLI, and pick it back up in Gemini CLI without losing context. + + + CASR extracts conversation context, code changes, and decisions into a + portable format that any agent can ingest. This enables provider-hopping + when you hit rate limits or want a second opinion. + + +
+ + } + title="Provider Agnostic" + description="Convert sessions between Claude, Codex, and Gemini" + gradient="from-violet-500/20 to-purple-500/20" + /> + } + title="Context Preservation" + description="Decisions, code changes, and rationale carry over" + gradient="from-blue-500/20 to-indigo-500/20" + /> + } + title="Seamless Handoff" + description="Resume mid-task without re-explaining context" + gradient="from-cyan-500/20 to-teal-500/20" + /> + } + title="Rate Limit Recovery" + description="Switch providers instantly when throttled" + gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-yellow-500/20" + /> + +
+
+ + + + {/* Section 2: Essential Commands */} +
} delay={0.2}> + + + + Use CASR when you hit a rate limit on one provider and want to seamlessly + continue your work on another without losing context. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 3: Common Scenarios */} +
} delay={0.3}> + +
+
+ ); +}

@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@ +'use client'; + +import { + Terminal, + Package, + Server, + GitBranch, + Play, + Shield, +} from 'lucide-react'; +import { + Section, + Paragraph, + CodeBlock, + TipBox, + Highlight, + Divider, + GoalBanner, + CommandList, + FeatureCard, + FeatureGrid, +} from './lesson-components'; + +export function DsrLesson() { + return ( +
+ + Ship releases locally when GitHub Actions is throttled with Doodlestein Self-Releaser. + + + {/* Section 1: What Is DSR */} +
} delay={0.1}> + + DSR (Doodlestein Self-Releaser) is fallback release + infrastructure for when GitHub Actions is throttled or unavailable. + It builds release artifacts locally using act (local GitHub Actions runner) + and publishes them directly. + + + When CI is backed up or your Actions minutes are exhausted, DSR lets you + cut a release from your local machine with the same reproducibility as CI. + + +
+ + } + title="Local Builds" + description="Run GitHub Actions workflows locally via act" + gradient="from-orange-500/20 to-red-500/20" + /> + } + title="Release Artifacts" + description="Build and publish release binaries" + gradient="from-blue-500/20 to-indigo-500/20" + /> + } + title="Cross-Platform" + description="Build for multiple OS targets from one machine" + gradient="from-green-500/20 to-emerald-500/20" + /> + } + title="CI Parity" + description="Same workflow definitions as your real CI" + gradient="from-purple-500/20 to-violet-500/20" + /> + +
+
+ + + + {/* Section 2: Essential Commands */} +
} delay={0.2}> + + + + DSR is a safety net, not a replacement for CI. Use it when Actions is down + or throttled and you need to ship now. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 3: Common Scenarios */} +
} delay={0.3}> + +
+
+ ); +}

@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@ +'use client'; + +import { + Terminal, + Search, + Zap, + Database, + Play, + Layers, +} from 'lucide-react'; +import { + Section, + Paragraph, + CodeBlock, + TipBox, + Highlight, + Divider, + GoalBanner, + CommandList, + FeatureCard, + FeatureGrid, +} from './lesson-components'; + +export function FsfsLesson() { + return ( +
+ + Search your local files at blazing speed with FrankenSearch's hybrid retrieval engine. + + + {/* Section 1: What Is FSFS */} +
} delay={0.1}> + + FSFS (FrankenSearch) is a two-tier hybrid local search engine + combining lexical (BM25) and semantic retrieval with progressive delivery. + It indexes your local files and returns results ranked by both keyword relevance + and meaning. + + + FSFS builds an index once, then serves queries in milliseconds. It supports + incremental updates so new files are searchable without a full re-index. + + +
+ + } + title="BM25 Lexical Search" + description="Fast keyword matching with TF-IDF scoring" + gradient="from-purple-500/20 to-indigo-500/20" + /> + } + title="Semantic Retrieval" + description="Embedding-based similarity for meaning-aware results" + gradient="from-blue-500/20 to-cyan-500/20" + /> + } + title="Progressive Delivery" + description="Results stream as they're found, fast first results" + gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-yellow-500/20" + /> + } + title="Incremental Index" + description="Only re-indexes changed files for fast updates" + gradient="from-green-500/20 to-emerald-500/20" + /> + +
+
+ + + + {/* Section 2: Essential Commands */} +
} delay={0.2}> + + + + Use FSFS when you need to search across large codebases or documentation sets + where simple grep isn't enough. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 3: Common Scenarios */} +
} delay={0.3}> + +
+
+ ); +}

@@ -0,0 +1,109 @@ +'use client'; + +import { + Terminal, + BellRing, + BookOpen, + RefreshCw, + Play, + FileText, +} from 'lucide-react'; +import { + Section, + Paragraph, + CodeBlock, + TipBox, + Highlight, + Divider, + GoalBanner, + CommandList, + FeatureCard, + FeatureGrid, +} from './lesson-components'; + +export function PcrLesson() { + return ( +
+ + Keep agents aligned after context compaction with Post-Compact Reminder. + + + {/* Section 1: What Is PCR */} +
} delay={0.1}> + + PCR (Post-Compact Reminder) is a Claude Code hook + that fires after context compaction and forces the agent to re-read AGENTS.md. + This prevents agents from “forgetting” project rules and conventions + after their context window is compressed. + + + Without PCR, agents that hit context limits lose awareness of project-specific + instructions. PCR ensures continuity by automatically injecting a reminder + to re-read the project guidelines. + + +
+ + } + title="Auto-Reminder" + description="Triggers on every context compaction event" + gradient="from-rose-500/20 to-pink-500/20" + /> + } + title="AGENTS.md Re-read" + description="Forces re-reading project guidelines" + gradient="from-blue-500/20 to-indigo-500/20" + /> + } + title="Context Recovery" + description="Restores lost project awareness after compaction" + gradient="from-green-500/20 to-emerald-500/20" + /> + } + title="Zero Config" + description="Installs as a Claude Code hook, works automatically" + gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-yellow-500/20" + /> + +
+
+ + + + {/* Section 2: Setup */} +
} delay={0.2}> + + + + PCR is especially important for long-running agent sessions that process + many files and are likely to hit context limits. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 3: How It Works */} +
} delay={0.3}> + +
+
+ ); +}

@@ -0,0 +1,109 @@ +'use client'; + +import { + Terminal, + HardDrive, + Shield, + Activity, + Play, + AlertTriangle, +} from 'lucide-react'; +import { + Section, + Paragraph, + CodeBlock, + TipBox, + Highlight, + Divider, + GoalBanner, + CommandList, + FeatureCard, + FeatureGrid, +} from './lesson-components'; + +export function SbhLesson() { + return ( +
+ + Protect your disk from pressure spikes with Storage Ballast Helper. + + + {/* Section 1: What Is SBH */} +
} delay={0.1}> + + SBH (Storage Ballast Helper) is a cross-platform disk-pressure + defense tool designed for AI coding workloads. It pre-allocates a “ballast” + file that can be released when disk space runs critically low, buying time + to clean up. + + + Agent coding sessions generate enormous amounts of build artifacts, logs, and + caches. SBH monitors disk usage and automatically releases ballast when pressure + exceeds configurable thresholds. + + +
+ + } + title="Ballast Files" + description="Pre-allocated space released under pressure" + gradient="from-blue-500/20 to-indigo-500/20" + /> + } + title="Monitoring" + description="Continuous disk usage tracking with alerts" + gradient="from-green-500/20 to-emerald-500/20" + /> + } + title="Auto-Release" + description="Ballast freed automatically at critical thresholds" + gradient="from-amber-500/20 to-orange-500/20" + /> + } + title="Safe Recovery" + description="Prevents out-of-space crashes and data loss" + gradient="from-red-500/20 to-rose-500/20" + /> + +
+
+ + + + {/* Section 2: Essential Commands */} +
} delay={0.2}> + + + + Create ballast files on machines running multi-agent swarms, where parallel + cargo builds and npm installs can exhaust disk space unexpectedly. + +
+ + + + {/* Section 3: Common Scenarios */} +
} delay={0.3}> + +
+
+ ); +}

@@ -329,7 +329,7 @@ test_integration() {
         local doctor_exit=0
 
         if command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
-            doctor_output=$(timeout "${ACFS_E2E_DOCTOR_TIMEOUT:-30}" env ACFS_DOCTOR_CI=true acfs doctor 2>&1)
